_comment: Agent review registry — source of truth for the agent-library 'reviewed' badge AND the leaderboard Agent Builder bonus. The app reads records with verdict:'reviewed'. Borderline/not-yet records are kept as a versioned review log. Human-readable analysis per agent lives in reviews/agents/<repo>.md.

quality.notes: Full Python package + Streamlit UI. Includes official GDPR text as reference data.
Multi-agent architecture: 6 specialised agents + 4 synthesisers across 8 frameworks.
Three-stage pipeline (normalise → design → IT validation) with thorough test coverage.
CLI + Streamlit web UI. GIAS v2024 8-step reasoning trail.
Audits other AI agents. Includes CLI + Claude Code slash command integration.
Single-file MVP — focused use case. Sample inputs included.

reviews.structure: Single-file Node.js HTTP app (ESM) with browser UI + node:test suite

quality.notes: Dependency-free Node app that fetches and categorises DNB/AFM/FD regulatory news into a weekly HTML briefing, with a browser form UI and 7 node:test unit tests covering parsing, categorisation, and reporting.

reviews.reasoning: Clears the bar: a real, runnable app (not a plan/README) with a clear interface, exported testable functions, and a 7-test suite using Node's built-in runner. Functionally exceeds thinner reviewed entries. Only minor gap is no dedicated samples directory (fixtures are inline), which does not block reviewed.

quality.notes: Deterministic GRC reporting agent as a proper Python package with CLI, 4 unit tests, synthetic sample datasets, and full demo outputs (HTML/JSON/markdown) plus a hash-chained audit trail.

reviews.reasoning: Real installable Python package (10 modules under src/, pyproject with a console-script entry point, stdlib-only) exposing a runnable CLI, backed by a tests/ suite covering normalisation, analysis traceability, and audit hash-chaining, with both sample inputs and sample outputs. On par with or above existing reviewed agents like RCM-Builder and CUEC_Crosscheck.

reviews.structure: Streamlit app + standalone CLI + FastAPI web app (bart/ sub-package)

quality.notes: IAM access-review agent cross-referencing AD vs HR exports into Claude-generated findings; ships a Streamlit app plus a polished CLI/FastAPI sub-package with 9 pytest tests, sample CSV/JSON data, a finding schema, and ISO 27001 / SOC 2 / ISAE 3402 control mappings.

reviews.reasoning: Comfortably clears the bar: real runnable agent code with multiple interfaces (Streamlit + CLI + FastAPI), 9 unit tests, sample inputs and outputs, dependency manifests, and an output JSON schema. The repo also serves as the broad Track 2 hub, but the actual agent implementation is substantive and well-structured.

quality.notes: Well-structured single-file CLI agent (~12 functions, batch Claude calls) turning a UC7 RCM into a GIAS-compliant work program with json/md/xlsx outputs; rich sample inputs and multi-model sample outputs (Haiku/Opus/Sonnet), but no unit tests.

reviews.reasoning: Meets the bar: a real, runnable agent with a clear CLI interface, dependency manifest, .env.example, thorough README, and strong sample inputs plus full multi-model sample outputs. Comparable to already-reviewed no-test entries like CUEC_Crosscheck and llm_owasp. Only gap is the absence of unit tests.

reviews.structure: Multi-module Python package + CLI (agent orchestrator, models/prompts, services/ extractors)

quality.notes: Clean multi-module Python CLI agent that extracts 6W-framework controls from PDF/DOCX policies via Claude with a Critic pass and JSON/XLSX output, but ships no tests, no committed sample fixtures (testdata/ is gitignored), and a stray 'docx_extractor copy.py' duplicate.

reviews.reasoning: Genuine, runnable multi-file agent — structurally stronger than some reviewed single-file MVPs — but has neither unit tests nor committed sample inputs/outputs.
reviews.pathToReviewed: Add a couple of unit tests, commit sample policy/output fixtures, and remove the 'docx_extractor copy.py' duplicate.

reviews.structure: FastAPI app (app/main.py + 3 services) with HTML/JS UI; run.py entry point

quality.notes: Runnable FastAPI web app that fills .docx templates via Claude with CSV/Excel ingestion, but no tests, no sample inputs/outputs, and a duplicated root-vs-app/ directory structure.

reviews.reasoning: Functional, well-structured real agent with a clear runnable interface, but no tests, no samples, and a duplicated root-level copy of the app/ package signalling unfinished cleanup.
reviews.pathToReviewed: Add unit tests and sample inputs/outputs; remove the duplicated root-level copy of app/services/templates.

reviews.structure: Multi-module Python package (agent/parsers/report) + Flask web UI

quality.notes: Substantial multi-module Flask agent that ingests historical audit reports (PDF/PPTX/XLSX/DOCX/images + SharePoint) and streams Claude-scored findings to an HTML report, but ships no tests, no sample inputs/outputs, and only a one-line README.

reviews.reasoning: Real, clearly-runnable agent with a richer codebase than several reviewed entries, but hits the 'functional but thin' combination: no tests AND no samples AND a single-sentence README.
reviews.pathToReviewed: Add a few sample fixtures and a proper usage README; add unit tests.

reviews.structure: Skeleton Python package (main.py + 7 workflow_steps/ modules + prompts.py) — all stubs

quality.notes: Well-architected 7-step plan with synthetic incident/context sample inputs, but every module is a docstring stub (functions return None / NotImplementedError) — no working code, no dependency manifest, no tests, no runnable interface.

reviews.reasoning: Below the bar: the SAAF plan template plus an unimplemented skeleton. main.py imports stub modules; all workflow_steps/*.py and prompts.py contain only docstrings and functions that return None or raise NotImplementedError; no requirements/pyproject and no runnable CLI/UI.
reviews.pathToReviewed: Implement the workflow_steps modules into working code, add a dependency manifest (requirements.txt/pyproject.toml), unit tests, and sample outputs.
